note: Hand-curated evergreen reading per value-chain layer. 'teaches' says what you understand about the industry's economics after reading it.

entries.title: Energy and AI
entries.teaches: The definitive baseline on how much electricity AI actually uses and will use — data-centre demand curves, grid constraints, and why energy is becoming the binding constraint on the whole stack.

entries.title: How to Build an AI Data Center
entries.teaches: The physical and economic anatomy of an AI data centre — power, cooling, construction timelines and cost structure — explained from first principles.

entries.title: Electricity Statement of Opportunities (ESOO)
entries.teaches: Australia's official grid demand forecast — where data-centre load shows up in the National Electricity Market, and how much headroom each state actually has for AI build-outs.

entries.title: AI Datacenter Energy Dilemma
entries.teaches: How AI demand translates into data-centre capacity, why power availability (not land or capital) gates the build-out, and how hyperscalers compete for grid connections.

entries.title: AI Neocloud Playbook and Anatomy
entries.teaches: The unit economics of renting out GPUs — cluster costs, utilisation, depreciation and payback periods. The best single explainer of how money is made (or lost) operating AI compute.

entries.title: Global Data Center Survey
entries.teaches: The annual ground truth on how data centres actually operate — costs, staffing, outages, densities — from the industry's own operators.

entries.title: Expectations of Data Centres and AI Infrastructure Developers
entries.publisher: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
entries.teaches: The social-licence rulebook — energy, water, community benefit, sovereignty — that every Australian data-centre project must satisfy to get approved.

entries.title: AI Chips: What They Are and Why They Matter
entries.teaches: The clearest primer on what makes a chip an 'AI chip' — why GPUs beat CPUs for AI economics, and why leading-edge fabrication concentrates in so few firms.

entries.title: Chip War: The Fight for the World's Most Critical Technology
entries.teaches: The geopolitical economics of the semiconductor supply chain — TSMC, ASML, export controls — and why chip access is a national-strategy question for countries like Australia.

entries.title: Navigating the High Cost of AI Compute
entries.teaches: How compute cost flows through the stack — what a GPU-hour costs, why demand outruns supply, and how that shapes who can afford to train and serve models.

entries.title: Data on the Trajectory of AI
entries.teaches: The measured trends behind model economics — training compute, cost per run, hardware efficiency — the empirical backbone for any claim about where frontier AI is heading.

entries.title: AI Index Report
entries.teaches: The annual end-to-end scorecard of the AI industry — investment, capability, adoption and cost trends in one sourced volume. The fastest way to calibrate the whole field once a year.

entries.title: LLMflation: LLM Inference Cost Is Falling Fast
entries.teaches: Why the price of intelligence keeps collapsing — inference cost curves and what 10x-per-year deflation means for who captures value between model labs and applications.

entries.title: AI's $600B Question
entries.teaches: The core tension of AI economics: the gap between what's being spent on infrastructure and the application revenue needed to justify it. The frame for judging every capex headline.

entries.title: Australia's Generative AI Opportunity
entries.teaches: The sizing of the prize for Australia — which sectors (healthcare, mining, professional services) capture generative-AI value and what adoption is worth to GDP by 2030.

entries.title: The Economic Potential of Generative AI
entries.teaches: The standard reference for where application-layer value pools sit — use cases by industry and function, and the productivity math behind the adoption story.

entries.title: National AI Plan
entries.publisher: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
entries.teaches: The Commonwealth's operating doctrine for AI — infrastructure, capability and diffusion. Most Australian AI policy announcements trace back to this document.
